etymology.description: 熱(熱) (rè, "heat; fervent") suggests a warm and intense feeling, which naturally conveys the sense of warmth. 烈(烈) (liè, "fierce; intense") enhances this idea, adding intensity and strength to emotions. Together, 熱烈(熱烈) (rèliè) originally conveys an intensely warm feeling that manifests as enthusiasm.
etymology.interpretation: For 'warm', the warmth and passion from 熱(熱) (rè, "heat") combined with the intensity from 烈(烈) (liè, "fierce") convey a heartfelt and engaging presence. For 'enthusiastic', the fierce intensity of 烈(烈) (liè, "intense") amplifies the fervent passion of 熱(熱) (rè, "fervent"), forming a mood of eager engagement and animated interest.

examples.chinese: 观众给予热烈的掌声(觀眾給予熱烈的掌聲)。
examples.english: The audience gave a warm applause.

examples.chinese: 他受到朋友的热烈欢迎(他受到朋友的熱烈歡迎)。
examples.pinyin: Tā shòudào péngyǒu de rèliè huānyíng.
examples.english: He received a warm welcome from his friends.

examples.chinese: 会议的气氛非常热烈(會議的氣氛非常熱烈)。
examples.english: The atmosphere of the meeting was very enthusiastic.
